_meta.purpose: Machine-readable knowledge pack consumed by Tier-1 adapters and Tier-2 templates, and to ground Tier-3 generation. Keys are neutral-IR / typical-source concepts; values are the authoritative CrewAI equivalents. Parsing of CrewAI-as-SOURCE lives in the CrewAI source adapter; this file also documents CrewAI as a conversion TARGET.

Graph_or_pipeline.import: from crewai import Crew, Process, Flow
Graph_or_pipeline.notes: A linear step pipeline maps to a sequential Crew. A graph with conditional edges, loops, or routers maps to a Flow that may drive one or more Crews. Do NOT flatten a branching graph into a linear Crew.

Crew.notes: A team of Agents executing a list of Tasks under a Process.

Flow.import: from crewai.flow.flow import Flow, start, listen, router, or_, and_
Flow.notes: Event-driven controller with @start/@listen/@router and typed/dict state. Use for explicit control flow.

ReactAgent.crewai_equivalent: one Agent + one Task in a small Crew
ReactAgent.import: from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
ReactAgent.notes: A single tool-looping LLM agent maps to a single Agent assigned to a single Task in a one-task Crew.

graph_state_TypedDict.crewai_equivalent: Flow state: a pydantic.BaseModel parameterising Flow[StateModel] (or an unstructured dict)
graph_state_TypedDict.import:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from crewai.flow.flow import Flow
graph_state_TypedDict.notes: Graph-level TypedDict state becomes the Flow state model accessed via self.state. Plain Crews have no global mutable state; data flows through Task outputs and context=[...].

task_output_dependency.notes: Data dependencies between steps are expressed by listing upstream Task objects in a downstream Task's context. This replaces reading fields off a shared state dict.
task_output_dependency.pattern: report_task = Task(description='...', expected_output='...', agent=writer, context=[coverage_task, redundancy_task])

Annotated_list_add.crewai_equivalent: plain list field on the Flow state model + .append()
Annotated_list_add.notes: Drop Annotated and operator.add entirely. Carry the list on the Flow state model and append directly.

node_function.crewai_equivalent: a Task executed by an Agent
node_function.return_convention: Do not return a dict. Define a Task with description + expected_output, assign an agent, and add it to a Crew. Use output_pydantic/output_json to get typed results.
node_function.pattern:
analyse_task = Task(
    description='Analyse the suite at {suite_path} and find gaps.',
    expected_output='A JSON object with a score and a list of gaps.',
    agent=analyst,
    context=[intake_task],
    output_pydantic=CoverageReport,
)
node_function.notes: expected_output is REQUIRED. Every Task must be added to a Crew(tasks=[...]) or produced inside a Flow method.

agent.pattern:
researcher = Agent(
    role='Senior Test Analyst',
    goal='Identify flaky and redundant tests',
    backstory='A meticulous QA engineer who reads CI history.',
    tools=[history_tool],
    llm=LLM(model='openai/gpt-4o'),
    allow_delegation=False,
    verbose=True,
)
agent.notes: role/goal/backstory are required and form the system prompt. An Agent does nothing until assigned to a Task in a Crew.

final_node.crewai_equivalent: the last Task in the Crew's task list (its output is the crew result)
final_node.notes: Set output_pydantic/output_json on the final Task to type the crew result. Access via result.pydantic / result.json_dict / result.raw.

sequential_edge.crewai_api: order of Tasks in Crew(tasks=[a, b, ...]) with Process.sequential
sequential_edge.notes: Tasks run in list order; each implicitly receives the previous task's output. There is no explicit edge API in a Crew.

dag_dependency.notes: Lists the specific upstream Task objects whose outputs are injected as context into the downstream Task.

conditional_edge.notes: Conditional branching is NOT possible in a plain Crew. Use a Flow. Every label a router can return must have a matching @listen.

cycle_loop.source_pattern: graph with a cycle (node A -> node B -> node A)
cycle_loop.crewai_pattern: Flow @router that loops back to an earlier method, guarded by a state counter
cycle_loop.notes: Always guard the loop with an iteration cap on the Flow state to guarantee termination.

fan_out_fan_in.crewai_api: Flow: multiple @listen on one trigger for fan-out; @listen(and_(a, b)) to join; or crew.kickoff_for_each(inputs=[...]) for data-parallel batches
fan_out_fan_in.notes: or_(a, b) fires when any trigger completes; and_(a, b) waits for all.

hierarchical_delegation.notes: A manager agent dynamically assigns/delegates tasks to worker agents instead of following static list order. Worker Tasks may omit agent=.

tool_decorator.crewai_equivalent: @tool from crewai.tools (returns a ready tool instance)
tool_decorator.registration: Pass the decorated object directly in Agent(tools=[...]) or Task(tools=[...]).
tool_decorator.notes: The decorated function IS the tool instance; pass it directly.

base_tool_class.import:
from crewai.tools import BaseTool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
base_tool_class.anti_pattern: Passing the class (CIHistoryTool) instead of an instance (CIHistoryTool()) in tools=[...]; or a BaseTool subclass never instantiated/assigned.
base_tool_class.notes: Always pass instances, not classes. args_schema arg names must match _run parameters.

prebuilt_tools.import: from crewai_tools import SerperDevTool, FileReadTool, ScrapeWebsiteTool
prebuilt_tools.notes: Requires the crewai-tools dependency. Some tools need their own API keys (e.g. SERPER_API_KEY).

human_input.pattern:
approve_task = Task(
    description='Propose which flaky tests to quarantine.',
    expected_output='A list of test ids to quarantine, with reasons.',
    agent=analyst,
    human_input=True,
)
human_input.flow_alternative: For programmatic HITL, run one crew, inspect result, then branch with a Flow @router on the human decision and kick off the next crew.
human_input.notes: human_input pauses at the TASK boundary after the agent responds; the human reviews/approves and feedback is fed back. Never implement HITL as auto-approve-only with real logic commented out.

checkpointing.note: CrewAI has NO MAF-style per-step checkpoint / resume-from-checkpoint. Map source checkpointers to Crew memory (cross-run recall), and model true pause/resume as separate crew.kickoff() calls orchestrated by a Flow.

memory.notes: Enables short-term (within run), long-term (persisted across runs, SQLite by default), and entity memory. This is recall, NOT a resumable step checkpoint.

knowledge.pattern:
policy = StringKnowledgeSource(content='Pinned tests are never removed.')
Crew(agents=[...], tasks=[...], knowledge_sources=[policy])
knowledge.notes: Attach reference material the crew can retrieve from during execution.

graph.invoke.notes: inputs fill {placeholders} in task descriptions. Read result.raw / result.pydantic / result.json_dict.

batched_invoke.notes: One crew run per input dict; returns a list of results.

providers.notes: CrewAI uses LiteLLM under the hood; the provider is selected by the model string on LLM(model=...). If no llm is passed to an Agent, the default model from environment is used. Prefer an explicit LLM(model=...).

reject_list.description: Any generated output containing the following is a conversion error.
items: Task constructed but never added to a Crew(tasks=[...]) or produced by a Flow
BaseTool subclass never instantiated or never assigned to an agent
passing a tool class instead of an instance in tools=[...]
plain sequential Crew used where source had conditional branching or loops (needs a Flow)
Flow @router label that no @listen responds to
HITL as auto-approve-only with real logic in comments
stdlib module in requirements.txt (json, os, io, sys, re, math, hashlib, pathlib)
